working: All three data sources are live this run (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC); CC pulled 100 campaigns with the newest send, Staff-Client eNews July 2026, landing 2026-07-16.

Email open rates stay well above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark: workspace average is 51.37%, and the Alumni segment leads with Alumni eNews July 2026 at 56.61% open and Alumni Reminder July at 45.52%.
Alumni sends keep driving the biggest measurable lift: Alumni eNews July 2026 (2026-07-09) lifted daily sessions +77.2% (14.5 to 25.7/day), and Staff-Client eNews June (2026-07-14) added +17.9%.
Organic Search remains a strong #2 channel at 33.5% (10,693 sessions) behind Direct at 50.8%, confirming SEO is carrying real volume.
not_working: Severe UTM leakage: Email is credited just 13 sessions (0.0%) across the whole window despite 317,113 total sends, while Direct sits at 50.8% (16,212). Every recent send shows email_sessions_3d of 0-4 vs direct_sessions_3d of 31-49, so GA4 is mis-crediting newsletter clicks as Direct.
Click performance is weak and softening: workspace avg click is 1.93% (down from 2.03% last run), and Staff-Client eNews July 2026 clicked only 0.19% on 8,904 sends despite a 31.97% open.
GSC is slipping: total clicks fell 25 to 21, impressions 3,448 to 3,148, and impression-weighted average position worsened from 18.64 to 20.06 since the 07-16 run.
Two of the newest sends underperform on open rate: Referring Partners eNews July at 21.29% and Staff-Client eNews July at 31.97%, both below the Alumni segment's 45-57%.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign) to every newsletter link. This is the single highest-value fix; it will move credit out of Direct and let us actually measure email's contribution.

Lean into the Alumni segment and event-driven content: Alumni sends produced the top recent lift (+77.2%) and highest opens (56.61%), and historically Alumni event eNews and 'Client eNews' drove the strongest clicks (Client eNews December at 10.17% click, +132% lift).
Fix the Staff-Client eNews CTA: 0.19% click on 8,904 sends is the weakest link performance of any recent send; tighten to one clear link/CTA and re-test.
Follow up on SEO slippage: average position dropped ~1.4 spots and clicks fell this week; review the location/near-me pages that lost impressions before position erodes further.
summary: All three sources are live: over the 1100-day window Direct leads at 50.8% and Organic Search at 33.5%, and the newest send (Staff-Client eNews July 2026, open 31.97%, click 0.19%, +1.5% lift) underlines the two standing problems, untagged newsletter links leaking into Direct and weak clicks, while GSC position slipped 18.6 to 20.1.
